scenario: Crescent Insurer runs Camunda 8.8 with 5 clusters and routes 4500 claims per day through a Decision Requirements Diagram that contains several decision tables and literal expressions. An operator navigates to the Decisions page in Operate and prepares to delete one decision definition that belongs to that DRD.
question: Which resource scope gets removed when an operator deletes that decision definition from the Decisions page?

options.text: Only the selected decision definition is removed; every other decision table or literal expression in the same DRD remains deployed
Only the version of the selected decision definition is removed; the DRD itself remains and other decision tables stay deployed
Only the selected decision definition and the literal expressions inside it are removed; other decision tables in the DRD remain
The whole DRD that contains the selected decision definition is removed, including every other decision table and literal expression in it

a.text: Incorrect. The docs state that deleting a decision definition deletes the DRD, which also takes down every other decision table and literal expression that is part of the same DRD.

b.text: Incorrect. The delete operation does not stop at the selected version; the docs say it removes the whole DRD that contains the decision definition the operator picked.

c.text: Incorrect. The deletion is not scoped to literal expressions only; the docs state that every other decision table and literal expression in the same DRD is also removed.

d.text: Correct. The Decisions page docs say deleting a decision definition deletes the DRD that contains it, so every other decision table and literal expression that is part of the DRD is removed at the same time.

explanation: Deleting a decision definition on the Decisions page deletes the entire DRD that contains it; every other decision table and literal expression that is part of that DRD is removed alongside it.
